visible_defects.defect: Dark staining on rafter and sheathing, ~2 ft diameter

vision_analysis.context_notes: Stain pattern radiates from a roof penetration (likely a vent pipe boot).

input.transcript: Attic — staining on the sheathing right where the plumbing vent comes through. Boot is probably cracked. Active leak.

expected.rationale: SoP §3.1: active leak indicators must be reported. Cracked vent boot is one of the most common, fixable roof leaks. Major because it's actively wetting structure.

vision_analysis.primary_subject: asphalt shingle roof viewed from ground with binoculars

visible_defects.defect: Heavy granule loss, several curling shingles at south-facing slope

vision_analysis.context_notes: Roof viewed from ground level — SoP doesn't require walking the roof.

input.transcript: Roof is original asphalt shingle, looks 20-plus years old. Granule loss is significant on the south side, some curling. Functional today but at end of expected life. Recommend a roofer evaluate before next winter.

expected.rationale: Aging roof, not yet failed. Monitor with timeframe is the right call. Don't predict service life specifically (SoP forbids that) but can note end-of-expected-life observation.

vision_analysis.primary_subject: single lifted shingle near roof edge

visible_defects.defect: Lifted shingle tab on edge of roof

vision_analysis.context_notes: One shingle tab visible from below.

input.transcript: Single shingle tab is lifted on the edge — looks like it was caught by wind. Easy fix. Glue it back down.

expected.rationale: One lifted tab is genuinely minor. Don't escalate to roofer evaluation unless inspector flagged it as a concern. Recommended action can be brief.
